pi_items.source: Official Docker Hub nginx image repository (https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx/). The alpine variant is a real, official Docker image with multiple versions available (mainline-alpine, 1-alpine, etc.). Confirmed via Docker documentation and official NGINX deployment guides.

Official NGINX documentation (https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/installing-nginx/installing-nginx-docker/) and Docker blog (https://www.docker.com/blog/how-to-use-the-official-nginx-docker-image/). The nginx:alpine image EXPOSE instruction specifies port 80 as the default HTTP port. This is confirmed as the standard default NGINX listening port for HTTP traffic.

Docker port mapping documentation (https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/container/run/). Port 8888 is a valid unprivileged host port that can be mapped to container port 80. This is an arbitrary choice by the task creator, but is valid for Docker port mapping syntax (host:container = 8888:80).

Docker container naming conventions. The name follows Docker's naming rules: uses lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens, max 63 characters. This is an arbitrary name chosen by the task creator and is syntactically valid for Docker container naming. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 29-30) and export_result.sh (line 22) where the container is referenced by this exact name.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real, official nginx:alpine Docker image from Docker Hub. The nginx:alpine image exposes port 80 as its default HTTP listening port, consistent with standard NGINX configuration. Port mapping 8888:80 (host:container) is a valid Docker configuration allowing access to the container's internal port 80 via the host's port 8888. The container name 'my-nginx-server' follows Docker naming conventions and is a valid, arbitrary identifier chosen by the task creator.
